extra.message: User-supplied 'name' is interpolated directly into a SQL INSERT string using Python's % string formatting operator: `"VALUES ('%(name)s')" % {'name': name}`. This bypasses parameterized query protection and allows SQL injection. An attacker can inject SQL by providing a name such as `'); DROP TABLE students; --`.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: Jinja2 is configured with `autoescape=False`, disabling HTML auto-escaping globally. Any template variable that renders user-controlled data without an explicit `| e` or `| escape` filter will result in reflected or stored XSS. Multiple templates render unsanitized user data (e.g., course.title, course.description, review.review_text, student.name in courses.jinja2/course.jinja2/students.jinja2) without escaping.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The template renders `review.review_text` without any escaping filter (`{{ review.review_text }}`). Because autoescape is disabled globally (sqli/app.py:35), attacker-controlled review text submitted via POST /courses/{id}/review is stored and rendered as raw HTML, enabling stored XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The template renders `course.description` without any escaping filter (`{{ course.description }}`). With autoescape disabled, any HTML/JS in an admin-supplied course description is rendered as raw HTML, enabling stored XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The template renders `course.title` and `course.description` without escaping filters in the courses listing. With autoescape=False, user-controlled course titles/descriptions stored in the database are output as raw HTML, enabling stored XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The template renders `course.title` without an escape filter (`{{ course.title }}`). With global autoescape disabled, a malicious course title stored in the database renders as raw HTML, enabling stored XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The `students` view accepts POST requests and creates new students (Student.create) without any authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can POST to /students/ to insert arbitrary student records. There is no `@authorize()` decorator or session check before the create call.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The `review` view accepts POST requests and creates course reviews (Review.create) without any authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can POST to /courses/{id}/review to inject arbitrary review text into the database. No `@authorize()` decorator is present.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The `csrf_middleware` is commented out in the application middleware stack (`# csrf_middleware,`). Although CSRF tokens are generated and included in forms, they are never validated server-side, making all state-changing POST endpoints vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks.

Passwords are hashed using MD5 (`md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()`). MD5 is cryptographically broken, has no salt, and is trivially reversed via rainbow tables or brute-force. Passwords should be hashed using bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: The aiohttp Application is created with `debug=True`. In debug mode the framework may expose stack traces, internal state, and enable additional diagnostic endpoints, disclosing sensitive information to attackers.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code
CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The Redis session storage is created with `httponly=False`. This means the session cookie is accessible from JavaScript, making it trivially stealable if an XSS vulnerability is exploited (which exists in this app due to disabled autoescape). The `httponly` flag should be set to `True`.

The 500-level error template renders `{{ error.__dict__ }}` — the full internal exception object dictionary — directly in the HTTP response. This exposes internal server details, stack state, and potentially configuration information (e.g., database DSN fragments) to clients.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The `evaluate` view accepts POST requests and creates Mark records (Mark.create) without any authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can POST to /students/{student_id}/evaluate/{course_id} to assign arbitrary grades to any student. No `@authorize()` decorator is present.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
